mechanism_narrative: Negative three-month change in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy stance that lowers the equity discount rate and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 twenty-day forward returns are not significantly positive during periods of negative three-month FEDFUNDS change over a three-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above seven point five percent signals substantial labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 twenty-day forward returns are positive in more than fifty-five percent of months when UNRATE exceeds seven point five percent over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production confirm a broad demand and supply recovery, raising earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO rise month-over-month, S&P 500 sixty-day forward returns are not significantly positive over a three-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis liquidity trap, a federal funds rate below twenty-five basis points combined with rising consumer prices drives real rates deeply negative, pushing capital into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL is rising, S&P 500 twenty-day forward returns are negative in more than fifty percent of triggered months over a two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing activity and downward earnings revisions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 five-day forward returns are positive in more than fifty-five percent of instances when INDPRO declines month-over-month over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand and supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 sixty-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than after negative growth over a three-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month nonfarm payroll growth exceeding one hundred fifty thousand signals labor market resilience and supports household income expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 five-day forward returns are negative in more than fifty percent of months when PAYEMS grows by more than one hundred fifty thousand over a two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales signal strong household-sector momentum and durable demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS rise month-over-month, S&P 500 five-day forward returns are not significantly positive over a three-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).
